commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows shared poll-intent detection to `pollQuestion` and `pollOption` and adds unit plus outbound send-validation coverage for schema-padded sends.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +40. Total +38 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the all-actions message schema exposes `pollDurationHours` and `pollMulti`, current main treats `pollDurationHours: 1` as poll intent, and `runMessageAction` rejects `send` before delivery.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review or Mantis/maintainer transport validation is needed because the remaining blocker is proof, not a mechanical code defect.

target.title: fix(outbound): stop schema-padded poll modifiers from blocking send
target.body:
## Summary

`hasPollCreationParams` previously treated **any** of the shared poll params (`pollQuestion`, `pollOption`, `pollDurationHours`, `pollMulti`) as poll intent if present with a non-zero / non-default value.

The shared `message` tool schema exposes those last two for both `send` and `poll` actions, and agent models routinely echo the schema's implied defaults (`1` for the `minimum: 1` integer, `false` for the boolean) on every plain `send` call alongside the rest of the schema-padded slots. The validator interpreted `pollDurationHours: 1` on its own as "the agent meant to create a poll", so `runMessageAction` threw:

> Poll fields require action \"poll\"; use action \"poll\" instead of \"send\".

ŌĆ”on routine sends, blocking thread replies from agents whose tool calls include the full schema-padded param surface.

This PR narrows the shared-param branch to the content-bearing fields (`pollQuestion`, `pollOption`). Channel-extra `poll*` params (`pollDurationSeconds`, `pollPublic`, `pollAnonymous`, ŌĆ”) remain strict because they aren't part of the shared tool-schema surface, so an explicit value still signals deliberate intent.

## What changed

- `src/poll-params.ts` ŌĆö split shared-param handling: only `pollQuestion` / `pollOption` count as standalone intent. Channel-extra detection is unchanged.
- `src/poll-params.test.ts` ŌĆö updated two existing assertions and added a new test for the schema-pad shape.
- `src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts` ŌĆö added an integration case asserting \`runMessageAction\` accepts a \`send\` with schema-padded \`pollDurationHours: 1\` / \`pollMulti: false\`.

## Behavior change for existing assertions

Two unit-level assertions changed semantics, both intentionally:

| Input | Old | New | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| \`{ pollMulti: true }\` alone | poll intent | not intent | A boolean modifier with no question or options is not a valid poll; in practice this combination only appears from schema-padding. |
| \`{ pollDurationHours: -1 }\` alone | poll intent | not intent | Same rationale. The shared-schema modifier alone is no longer enough. |

Channel-extra equivalents (\`pollAnonymous: true\`, \`pollPublic: true\`, \`pollDurationSeconds: -5\`) are unchanged and still count as intent, because they are not part of the schema-padded surface that agents echo on every send.

## Real behavior proof

Observed on an OpenClaw agent (v2026.5.28) configured with the Slack channel. The agent was @mentioned in a thread and emitted a tool call from \`openrouter/auto\` of the form:

\`\`\`json
{
  \"action\":\"send\", \"channel\":\"slack\", \"target\":\"channel:CŌĆ”\", \"message\":\"<@UŌĆ”> hi\",
  \"threadId\":\"ŌĆ”\",
  \"pollQuestion\":\"\", \"pollOption\":[], \"pollDurationHours\":1, \"pollMulti\":false,
  ŌĆ”
}
\`\`\`

This pattern appeared 10├Ś in a single session transcript on the affected agent. \`runMessageAction\` rejected every one with \`Poll fields require action \"poll\"ŌĆ”\`, so no replies ever made it to Slack.

I patched the equivalent guard in the running container's bundled \`message-action-runner-*.js\` to require a non-empty \`pollQuestion\` (or non-empty \`pollOption\`) before throwing ŌĆö same intent as this PR but inline at the call site. After the patch + container restart, the same tool-call shape went through cleanly and the agent replied in-thread as expected (Slack message ID returned by the workspace plugin; gateway logs show \`socket mode connected\` then a \`message\` outbound with no validator error).

Targeted local tests on this PR (Vitest 4.1.7, node 22.18):

\`\`\`
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs --changed origin/main
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 15.22s
\`\`\`

The new integration assertion (\"allows send when only schema-padded shared poll modifiers are present\") is part of the 24 in \`vitest.infra.config.ts\`. The regression case in \`poll-params.test.ts\` (\"does not treat shared modifier params ŌĆ” as poll creation intent without a question or options\") is part of the 11 in \`vitest.unit-fast.config.ts\`.

Not tested locally: full repo \`pnpm check\` (delegated to a remote provider in this repo) and \`pnpm build\`. Happy to address CI follow-ups.

## AI assistance disclosure

- [x] AI-assisted (Claude / Claude Code). I read the failing transcript, the validator, the tool-schema callsite, and the existing tests; chose the narrowing scope; wrote the fix and tests; ran the targeted Vitest shards.
- [x] Real-behavior proof above is from my own OpenClaw deployment, not from AI-generated mocks.
- [x] I understand what the code does ŌĆö the change is a strict subset of the previous logic: any input that returned \`true\` before still returns \`true\`, *except* shared-modifier-only inputs that match the schema-pad shape.
target.author: codezz
